description_long: This object consists of three large, rounded mushroom caps with a dark brown base color and numerous large, irregular white spots scattered across their surfaces. The largest cap is elevated above the ground by a thick, dark stem, while two smaller caps are attached at lower heights, each supported by their own stems. The caps have a smooth, slightly matte finish and their margins are gently curved, giving them a natural, organic appearance. The stems are slender and dark, contrasting with the lighter, spotted caps. The overall structure resembles a cluster of mushrooms, commonly found in parks or landscaped urban green spaces.
description: A cluster of three dark brown mushroom caps with large white spots, supported by slender dark stems. The caps are smooth and gently curved, forming a natural, organic group.
description_view_0: Front view showing the largest cap above two smaller, spotted caps, all supported by dark stems.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the relative heights and arrangement of the three caps, with the largest cap most prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view with the largest cap mostly obscuring the stems and smaller caps, emphasizing the cap's broad surface.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the curvature of the stems and the spatial relationship between the three caps.
